records.summary: RegistrationAdmissionSupportMaterializer owns key, value, registry-parameter, and recognition-open-seam support records for registration admissions.
records.rationale: Registration products need source-backed key/value/parameter facts before DI world construction can spend them. This support materializer is the lower publication boundary beneath admission product classification, so future work should not hand-spell registration key/value source records or DI key identities elsewhere.
guidance: Start here when registration key/value/registry-parameter source records, DI key identities, field provenance, or recognition open seams change.
Keep key provenance separate from provider-key provenance; registry lookup keys such as `Registration.defer(...)` are not ordinary admitted-key claims.
Use kernel DI key identity records instead of hiding key semantics in descriptions or local strings.
Constructable DI key identities must come from evaluator- or checker-proven runtime value shape and should carry a TypeScript declaration identity when the declaration is source-backed.

anchors.summary: Support record materializer for registration admission observations.
Kernel DI key identity seed taxonomy, including evaluator-proven constructable keys.
Registration kernel boundary and support-materializer split.

records.summary: RegistrationKernelEmitter owns registration admission product classification, source observation framing, claims, materialized-product envelopes, and batch emission.
records.rationale: The emitter is the registration kernel boundary and should remain the single place where source observations become typed admission products and durable kernel records. Configuration may own a concrete admission scope and DI later spends products, but the admission product shape belongs here.
guidance: Start here when registration admission product classes, strategy-to-product-kind mapping, admission claims, or batch framing changes.
Keep support records in RegistrationAdmissionSupportMaterializer and runtime container effects in DI world construction.
Preserve scope-owned emission: source-module inquiry products and configuration-step-owned admission products are related but not interchangeable.
